overview: The DTD Filter processes DTD (Document Type Definition) documents, specifically targeting XML-DTDs that contain translatable text entity declarations. A primary use case is Mozilla DTD files that accompany XUL documents used to build user-interface components. The filter extracts entity declaration values as translatable text units and supports inline code detection via regular expressions.

useCodeFinder.description: When enabled, activates pattern-based detection of inline codes within extracted text. This allows regular expressions defined in the code finder rules to identify spans of text (such as variables or placeholders) that should be treated as inline codes rather than translatable content. For example, enabling this with an appropriate rule can protect variable references like 'VAR1' from modification during translation.
notes: Used in conjunction with codeFinderRules to define the patterns that identify inline codes. The rules use the #v1 format with count and rule entries.
Regular expressions in YAML notation require double-escaping of backslashes (e.g., \bVAR\d\b becomes \\bVAR\\d\\b in YAML).

moveLeadingAndTrailingCodesToSkeleton.description: When enabled, inline codes that appear at the beginning or end of a segment are moved to the skeleton (non-translatable framework) rather than being included as part of the translatable content. This can simplify segments for translators by removing codes that bookend the text.

mergeAdjacentCodes.description: When enabled, consecutive inline codes that appear next to each other are merged into a single placeholder. This reduces the number of individual code placeholders translators need to manage when multiple codes appear in sequence.

simplifierRules.description: Rules for simplifying the representation of inline codes in extracted text. These rules define how complex inline code patterns can be reduced to simpler placeholder representations for easier handling during translation.

processingNotes: Input encoding is determined by BOM detection first; if no BOM is present, the default encoding specified in the filter options is used.
For UTF-8 output: a BOM is included only if the input was also UTF-8 and had a BOM. If converting from a non-UTF-8 encoding to UTF-8, no BOM is written.
Line-break style in the output matches the original input document's line-break type.

examples.title: DTD with translatable entity declarations
examples.description: Shows a typical DTD file with text entity declarations where the entity values are extracted as translatable text.
examples.output: The entity values 'Find Files', 'File', and 'Edit' are each extracted as individual translatable text units.

examples.title: Inline code detection with useCodeFinder
examples.description: Demonstrates using useCodeFinder and codeFinderRules to protect variable references (e.g., VAR1) as inline codes. The regular expression \bVAR\d\b matches variable patterns, but backslashes must be double-escaped in YAML notation.
examples.input:
useCodeFinder: true
codeFinderRules: "#v1\ncount.i=1\nrule0=\\bVAR\\d\\b"

<!ENTITY dialog.fileCount "Number of files = VAR1">
examples.output: The text 'VAR1' within the entity value is marked as an inline code placeholder, protecting it from modification during translation.
